text:
e
word, meaning the country or its people, is derived from Adarsa. It
was regarded as the western boundary of Aryavarta. According to
another view, it lay not far from the ancient kingdoms of Srughna
and Trigarta (Kangra).
Trigarta denotes the country drained by the three rivers, Ravi,
Beas and Sutlej. It comprised the whole of the upper Doab bet-
ween the Ravi and Sutlej. Hemacandra identifies Trigarta with
Jalandhara.
Cipiiandsikas are those with Mongolian features such as flat
nose. The Periplus mentions a people called Girrhadae with flattened
nose, who, according to SchofF, were a Bhota tribe, whose descen-
dants, still called Kirata, live in the Morung, west ofSikkim.
Dasitakas may be an aboriginal tribe. Panini (IV. 1.131)
derives the word from Dasi, a slave woman.
The are located, according to the Mnrkandeya-purdna,
between the Bahlikas and Abhiras. According to Pargiter, Vata-
dhana was a country on the eastern side of the Sutlej, southwards
from Ferozepur.
